title: Tundra entry expansion phase
summary: Tundra now has its wet thaw-edge cluster, but the exposed half still reads thinner than the other live biomes. This wave adds one cushion flower, one tundra lichen, one alpine bird, and one freeze-thaw landmark so `wind-bluff`, `snow-meadow`, and `frost-ridge` feel as authored as the right half.

execution_order.goal: Prepare tundra entry expansion handoff with science-safe candidates.
execution_order.why_now: Tundra is the thinnest biome; it needs more entries to feel complete.
More entries means more to discover, compare, and teach.
Science accuracy is a hard gate.

guardrails: All entries must be real alpine/arctic tundra species or habitat features.
At least one new entry should be a collectible species kids can discover.
Authored entities must be positioned to teach zonation (snow-meadow, thaw-skirt, frost-ridge).
Do not change existing entry IDs, zone boundaries, or platform layouts.
Favor the exposed left half of tundra over adding more wet-edge carriers to meltwater-edge.
Keep the lichen scientific label genus-level unless an exact species is source-matched during implementation.
If an alpine plant or lichen is shared with treeline, keep it in `src/content/shared-entries.ts` so both biomes still resolve to one journal entry.
